title: Literals should be avoided for promise rejection
description: Literals should not be used for promise rejectionAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalreliabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?More InfoThe use of literals (primitive values such as strings, numbers, booleans, etc.) for promise rejection is generally discouraged. While it is syntactically valid to provide literals as a rejected promise value, it is considered best practice to use instances of the Error class or its subclasses instead.Using an instance of the Error class allows you to provide more meaningful information about the error. The Error class and its subclasses provide properties such as message and stack that can be used to convey useful details about the error, such as a description of the problem, the context in which it occurred, or a stack trace for debugging.new Promise
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.
